title: Eremanthus goyazensis
intro: Eremanthus goyazensis, also known as Brazilian feather top, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eremanthus goyazensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eremanthus goyazensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, bronchitis, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Eremanthus goyazensis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This family consists of over 23,000 species, and Asteraceae is one of the largest and most diverse plant families. Eremanthus goyazensis is a flowering plant native to Brazil, and its classification within the family is further supported by its characteristic flower and leaf structures. The genus Eremanthus is a relatively small group within the Asteraceae family, comprising around 10 species. Further classification of Eremanthus goyazensis may include its species rank and any potential sub-classifications within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Eremanthus goyazensis has been found to possess various medicinal properties, with its extracts showing potential in treating inflammation and oxidative stress.
